title: useLocalStorage hook
description: Generic React hook that syncs state to localStorage. Handles SSR-safe initialization and JSON serialization. Returns a tuple just like useState.

title: useDebounce hook
description: Delays updating a value until after a specified wait time. Useful for search inputs to avoid firing API calls on every keystroke.

title: useIntersectionObserver hook
description: Tracks whether a DOM element is visible in the viewport. Use for lazy loading images or triggering animations on scroll.

title: TypeScript discriminated union
description: Model async states (loading / success / error) as a discriminated union. TypeScript automatically narrows the type in each switch branch — no casting needed.

title: TypeScript satisfies operator
description: The satisfies operator validates that a value matches a type WITHOUT widening the inferred type. You get both type checking AND narrowed autocomplete.

title: TypeScript mapped type — make all fields optional deeply
description: Recursively makes every field in a nested type optional. Useful for update payloads or config merging where you only patch changed fields.

title: Next.js server action with Zod validation
description: Next.js 14+ server action that validates form data with Zod before saving. Returns field-level errors on failure instead of throwing.

title: Next.js generateMetadata for dynamic routes
description: Dynamic metadata generation for Next.js App Router. generateMetadata runs on the server at request time and shares data fetching with the page component.

title: Next.js route handler with streaming
description: Next.js route handler that streams a response using the Web Streams API. Good for LLM output or large file downloads.

title: TanStack Query with optimistic update
description: TanStack Query v5 optimistic update pattern. Immediately updates the cache, rolls back on error, and refetches on settle to sync with server.

title: Tailwind CSS clsx + tailwind-merge utility
description: The canonical cn() utility used in Shadcn/UI. clsx handles conditional classes, tailwind-merge resolves Tailwind conflicts (e.g., px-2 + px-4 = px-4).

title: Tailwind CSS responsive grid with auto-fill
code:
...
...
description: Two grid patterns: breakpoint-based responsive columns with Tailwind classes, and CSS auto-fill for truly fluid grids where column count depends on available space.

title: Tailwind CSS dark mode with next-themes
description: Set up Tailwind dark mode with next-themes. suppressHydrationWarning prevents the React warning when the class is added before hydration.

title: Tailwind CSS animate-in with tailwindcss-animate
code:
Content
Modal
Leaving
description: CSS animations using the tailwindcss-animate plugin (bundled with Shadcn). Compose direction, type, duration. Pair with Radix exit animations via data-[state=closed].

title: React Hook Form with Zod resolver
description: React Hook Form integrated with Zod for schema-based validation. zodResolver infers types from the schema so FormData is always in sync with validation rules.

title: CSS scroll snap carousel
description: Pure CSS touch-friendly carousel using scroll-snap. No JS required. Works on mobile with momentum scrolling. Hide the scrollbar for a cleaner look.

title: CSS container queries
description: Container queries let components respond to their parent's size instead of the viewport. The card can be 3-column in a wide sidebar but 1-column in a narrow widget.

title: CSS custom properties with JS interop
description: CSS custom properties (variables) defined as raw HSL values (no hsl() wrapper) so they can be used with opacity modifiers. JS can update them at runtime for theming.

title: Git — undo last commit, keep changes staged
code:
# Undo the last commit but keep all changes staged (soft reset)
git reset --soft HEAD~1

# Undo last commit, unstage everything (keep files modified)
git reset HEAD~1

# Undo last commit AND discard all changes (DESTRUCTIVE)
git reset --hard HEAD~1

# Fix the last commit message without changing code
git commit --amend -m "correct message"
description: Three flavors of undoing a commit. --soft keeps staged, --mixed unstages but keeps files, --hard wipes everything. Use --amend to fix a commit message.

title: Git — interactive rebase to squash commits
code:
# Squash last 3 commits into one
git rebase -i HEAD~3

# In the editor that opens:
# pick abc1234 First commit
# squash def5678 Second commit    ← change 'pick' to 'squash'
# squash ghi9012 Third commit     ← change 'pick' to 'squash'

# Save. Git opens another editor to write the combined commit message.

# Push (force required because history changed)
git push --force-with-lease origin feature-branch
description: Squash multiple commits into one clean commit before merging a PR. Use --force-with-lease instead of --force to avoid overwriting others' pushed work.

title: Git — stash with a message and pop selectively
code:
# Stash with a descriptive message
git stash push -m "WIP: half-done review page"

# List all stashes
git stash list
# stash@{0}: On main: WIP: half-done review page
# stash@{1}: On dev: experiment with grid layout

# Apply a specific stash without removing it
git stash apply stash@{1}

# Pop (apply and remove) the most recent stash
git stash pop
description: Named stashes make it easy to track multiple work-in-progress states. Use stash list to inspect, apply to try without consuming, pop to finish.

title: Git — cherry-pick a commit to another branch
code:
# Copy a single commit from another branch
git checkout main
git cherry-pick abc1234

# Cherry-pick a range (abc up to and including def)
git cherry-pick abc1234..def5678

# Cherry-pick without committing (stage the changes only)
git cherry-pick --no-commit abc1234
description: Copy specific commits from one branch to another without merging the whole branch. Useful for backporting a hotfix to an older release branch.

title: Python — dataclass with post-init validation
description: Python dataclass with __post_init__ for field validation. Cleaner than a regular class but still enforces invariants. The dataclass decorator auto-generates __init__ and __repr__.

title: Python — list comprehension vs generator for memory
code:
# List comprehension — builds entire list in memory
squares_list = [x**2 for x in range(1_000_000)]  # uses ~8 MB

# Generator expression — lazy, one item at a time
squares_gen = (x**2 for x in range(1_000_000))   # uses ~200 bytes

# Use generators when you only iterate once
total = sum(x**2 for x in range(1_000_000))  # memory efficient

# Use lists when you need random access or multiple passes
scores = [compute(item) for item in dataset]
print(scores[0], max(scores))
description: Generators are lazy iterators — they compute values on demand without storing them all at once. Prefer generators in pipelines or sum/any/all. Use lists when you need indexing.

title: Python — functools.lru_cache for memoization
description: lru_cache caches function results by arguments. Turns exponential fibonacci into linear. maxsize=None for unlimited cache. Only works with hashable arguments.

title: Python — context manager with contextlib
code:
from contextlib import contextmanager

@contextmanager
def timer(label: str):
    import time
    start = time.perf_counter()
    try:
        yield
    finally:
        elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start
        print(f'{label}: {elapsed:.4f}s')

# Usage
with timer('TF-IDF vectorize'):
    vectors = engine.vectorize(documents)
description: Create a context manager from a generator function using contextlib. The code before yield runs on __enter__, after yield runs on __exit__ (even if an exception occurs).

title: FastAPI dependency injection
description: FastAPI's Depends() system for dependency injection. Dependencies are called automatically, can be nested, and share instances within a request. lru_cache makes settings a singleton.

title: FastAPI background tasks
description: FastAPI BackgroundTasks runs a function after the response is already sent to the client. Good for non-blocking work like sending emails or logging analytics.

title: Pydantic v2 model with validators
description: Pydantic v2 model with field-level validators. @field_validator replaces the v1 @validator syntax. @classmethod is required in v2. Use @model_validator for cross-field validation.

title: Python — enumerate and zip patterns
code:
# enumerate: index + value without a counter variable
fruits = ['apple', 'banana', 'cherry']
for i, fruit in enumerate(fruits, start=1):
    print(f'{i}. {fruit}')

# zip: iterate two lists in parallel
names = ['Alice', 'Bob']
scores = [95, 87]
for name, score in zip(names, scores):
    print(f'{name}: {score}')

# zip with strict=True to catch length mismatch (Python 3.10+)
for a, b in zip(names, scores, strict=True):
    print(a, b)
description: enumerate() adds an index to any iterable without needing a counter. zip() pairs items from multiple iterables. strict=True raises ValueError if lengths differ.

title: React — useMemo vs useCallback
description: useMemo caches a computed value, useCallback caches a function reference. Both are only useful when the result is passed to a memo'd child or used as a dependency.

title: React — useReducer for complex state
description: useReducer for state machines with multiple related values. Typed Action union prevents invalid dispatch calls. Prefer useReducer over multiple useState when states interact.

title: React — createContext with a custom hook
description: Context + custom hook pattern. Initialize context as null and throw in the hook if used outside a provider — catches errors at runtime instead of silently returning undefined.

title: CSS — logical properties for RTL support
description: CSS logical properties replace left/right/top/bottom with start/end/block/inline — relative to writing mode. Use them for automatic RTL and vertical writing support.

title: CSS — :has() parent selector
description: CSS :has() is a parent selector — style an element based on its children. Supported in all modern browsers. Replaces a lot of JavaScript-driven class toggling.

title: CSS — clip-path for geometric shapes
description: clip-path crops an element to a polygon or shape. The polygon() function takes percentages relative to the element's bounding box. Use Clippy tool online to generate coordinates.

title: TypeScript — template literal types
description: Template literal types create string types by interpolation. Combine with Capitalize, Uppercase, and mapped types to generate handler names, route types, or accessor patterns.

title: TypeScript — infer keyword in conditional types
description: The infer keyword captures a type from within a conditional type pattern. Used to build utility types like ReturnType, Parameters, and Awaited that ship in TypeScript's stdlib.

title: Next.js — parallel routes and intercepting routes
description: Parallel routes (@slot) render multiple pages in the same layout. Intercepting routes (.) show a route as an overlay (e.g., image gallery modal) without losing the current page.

title: Next.js — revalidatePath and revalidateTag
description: revalidatePath purges the cache for a specific URL. revalidateTag purges all cached fetches with a given tag. Call them from server actions after mutations.

title: Python — walrus operator for assignment in conditions
code:
import re

# Without walrus (compute twice)
if re.search(r'\bdef\b', code):
    match = re.search(r'\bdef\b', code)
    print(match.group())

# With walrus operator (compute once)
if match := re.search(r'\bdef\b', code):
    print(match.group())

# In a while loop — read until empty
while chunk := file.read(8192):
    process(chunk)

# In list comprehension — filter and transform in one pass
results = [y for x in data if (y := transform(x)) is not None]
description: The walrus operator (:=) assigns and evaluates in one expression. Avoids computing a value twice for both the condition check and the body. Python 3.8+.

title: Python — sorted with key function and operator.attrgetter
code:
from operator import attrgetter, itemgetter
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Snippet:
    title: str
    ease_factor: float
    next_review: str

snippets = [Snippet('B', 2.5, '2026-04-22'), Snippet('A', 1.8, '2026-04-20')]

# Sort by attribute
by_review = sorted(snippets, key=attrgetter('next_review'))

# Sort by multiple attributes
by_ease_title = sorted(snippets, key=attrgetter('ease_factor', 'title'))

# Sort dict list by key
records = [{'score': 0.8}, {'score': 0.9}]
by_score = sorted(records, key=itemgetter('score'), reverse=True)
description: Use operator.attrgetter for objects and itemgetter for dicts as sort keys. They're faster than lambdas and more readable. Pass multiple attributes for stable multi-key sort.

title: Python — collections.Counter for frequency analysis
code:
from collections import Counter

words = ['react', 'typescript', 'react', 'python', 'react', 'typescript']
counts = Counter(words)
print(counts)                # Counter({'react': 3, 'typescript': 2, 'python': 1})
print(counts.most_common(2)) # [('react', 3), ('typescript', 2)]

# Combine counters
tags_a = Counter(['react', 'hooks'])
tags_b = Counter(['react', 'python'])
print(tags_a + tags_b)  # Counter({'react': 2, 'hooks': 1, 'python': 1})

# Count characters in a string
char_freq = Counter('hello world')
description: Counter is a dict subclass for counting hashable objects. most_common(n) returns the n most frequent items. Add/subtract counters directly. Useful for TF computation.

title: Python — defaultdict to avoid KeyError
code:
from collections import defaultdict

# Group snippets by language
snippets = [
    {'title': 'useEffect', 'language': 'typescript'},
    {'title': 'sm2()', 'language': 'python'},
    {'title': 'useCallback', 'language': 'typescript'},
]

by_language = defaultdict(list)
for s in snippets:
    by_language[s['language']].append(s['title'])

print(dict(by_language))
# {'typescript': ['useEffect', 'useCallback'], 'python': ['sm2()']}

# defaultdict(int) for counting
word_count = defaultdict(int)
for word in 'the cat sat on the mat'.split():
    word_count[word] += 1
description: defaultdict provides a default value for missing keys instead of raising KeyError. Common factory functions: list (grouping), int (counting), set (deduplication).

title: TypeScript — Extract and Exclude utility types
description: Extract keeps union members that match, Exclude removes them. Combined with conditional types they let you filter object keys or union variants programmatically.

title: CSS — aspect-ratio property
description: aspect-ratio replaces the old padding-top percentage hack for maintaining proportions. The browser automatically sets the other dimension. Works on replaced and block elements.

title: React — forwardRef with TypeScript
description: forwardRef lets parent components attach a ref to an inner DOM element. Always set displayName so the component shows correctly in React DevTools.

title: Git — bisect to find which commit introduced a bug
code:
# Start bisect
git bisect start

# Tell Git: current state is broken
git bisect bad

# Tell Git: this old commit was known good
git bisect good v1.0.0

# Git checks out a middle commit — test it
# If bug is present:
git bisect bad
# If bug is absent:
git bisect good

# Git narrows down until it prints:
# abc1234 is the first bad commit

# Always end bisect when done
git bisect reset
description: git bisect does a binary search through commit history to find which commit introduced a bug. If you have 1000 commits, it takes only ~10 steps to find the culprit.

title: Python — type narrowing with TypeGuard
description: TypeGuard is a return type annotation that tells the type checker a function narrows the type of its argument. Lets you write custom isinstance-style checks with full type narrowing.

title: Supabase — real-time subscription with cleanup
description: Supabase real-time subscription with a cleanup function to unsubscribe when the component unmounts. Filters by user_id so clients only receive their own data.
